title: test: reduce brittle catalog assertions
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Replaces copied catalog values in Enter/Gen tests with registry- and catalog-derived contract assertions.
- Keeps billing and safety contracts explicit: positive configured rates, capability mapping, restricted model filtering, and safety cache versioning.
- Leaves unrelated untracked pnpm files out of the branch.

Validation:
- npm run decrypt-vars
- npx biome check --write <touched files>
- enter unit slice: 237 passed
- enter integration slice: 71 passed
- gen focused slice: 61 passed
- git diff --check

gist.headline: Sweeping the test suite
gist.blurb: We've cleaned up our test suite to use dynamic catalog assertions instead of hardcoded values. This makes our internal checks much more robust as we add new models.
gist.summary: Replaced hardcoded catalog values in the Enter and Gen test suites with dynamic registry-derived assertions to reduce brittleness.
gist.impact: This internal refactor ensures our test suite automatically adapts to new models and pricing changes without requiring manual updates.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny janitor's hat, sweeping up broken pixelated code blocks (representing brittle tests) in a cozy server room.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny janitor's hat, sweeping up broken pixelated code blocks with a small broom. A chunky retro server rack glows softly in the background. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
